content: Parkinson's disease (PD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder characterized by loss of dopaminergic neurons in the substantia nigra pars compacta and presence of Lewy bodies (alpha-synuclein aggregates). Motor features (cardinal signs): resting tremor (4-6 Hz, pill-rolling), rigidity (cogwheel), bradykinesia (slowness, decrement on repetitive movements), and postural instability (late feature). Diagnosis requires bradykinesia plus at least one of tremor, rigidity, or postural instability. Non-motor features often precede motor symptoms: hyposmia, REM sleep behavior disorder, constipation, depression, anxiety. Disease progression follows Braak staging (olfactory bulb → brainstem → midbrain → cortex). Genetic forms (10%): SNCA, LRRK2, PARKIN, PINK1 mutations. Pathophysiology involves mitochondrial dysfunction, oxidative stress, impaired protein clearance, and neuroinflammation. Differential diagnosis includes essential tremor, drug-induced parkinsonism, atypical parkinsonism (MSA, PSP, CBD).
